name: enrich_fk_helpers
details: enriches fk helper columns
frames_in: frames containing fk references, target frames of the reference
frames_out: frames containing fk reference
meta_workflow_prerequisites: the target frames of the foreign key references must know which column is the pk and they must contain a helper policy (which columns are allowed as a pk helper, which ones are the default), the frames containing the fk references, must contain information which column is a fk and which table they reference
meta_or_config_input: a list of the fk columns that should be enriched each with the desired target fields (optionally – otherwise the default is used) identified by frame / column. Shorthand options should be available to enrich all fk columns for a frame or workbook with the default helpers.
meta_output_content: each column that has been enriched recieves an attribute indicating that it s a helper column.
meta_output_consumer: the render layer can assign differnet styling or write protection to helper columns.The inverse projection `drop_helpers` relies on the helper attributes to know which collumns to remove.
meta_output_loss_risks: If the meta output is lost before the rendering layer the user editing the workbook might misstake the helper column for real data and use it for an input.If `drop_helpers` does not find the appropopriate attributes the helper columns - which might have been edited – are written as structured data creating redundancy and conflicts in the stored data.

name: drop_helpers
details: drops the fk helper columns
frames_in: The remvoal of the helper columns applies to the whole workbook.
frames_out: All frames in the workbook.
meta_or_config_input: The remvoal of the helper columns applies to the whole workbook, so there is no real input required

name: helper_config
details: applies a heuristic to derive a schema description (and thus fk policies)
frames_in: This can be run on the whole workbook or on a list of frames, so the input frames have to be configured.
frames_out: All frames the transformation is applied to
meta_or_config_input: The heuristic will use parameters and provide a default, that can be overwritten globally or per frame: listof column titles identified as pk in order of relevance (default: [“ID”, “id”, “PK”, “pk”, “name”]), pattern for fk columns (default: [“ID_<reference>”, “ID (<reference>)”,“id_<reference>”, “id (<reference>)”])
meta_output_content: PK column per frame / FK columns per frame / target Frame for each FK column / allowed helper columns for each frame
meta_output_loss_risks: transformations requiring the schema informaitons might fail.

name: compact_codec
details: compact multiple values / columns to one
frames_in: frame that contains the specific column that is subject to the compaction / expansion
frames_out: the operation happens in place the input frame(s) are the output frames
meta_or_config_input: information that is needed to perform the compaction / expansion like separators, mode choices in case we can select between mutliple compaction algorithms etc. is supplied as config
meta_output_content: details on the compaction like separators or compaction strategy choosen

name: expand_codec
details: expand one column to multiple values
frames_in: frame that contains the specific column that is subject to the compaction / expansion
frames_out: the operation happens in place the input frame(s) are the output frames

name: xref_crosstable
details: project a tuple relation (xref) to a pivot like projection (crosstable)
frames_in: the frames containing the entities involved and a potentially preexisting relation table in xref form.
frames_out: the relation in crosstable form is generated. the relation table in xref form is generated if it does not exist yet (first roundtrip).
meta_or_config_input: The 2 entities that are involved in the relation, a selection of instances or a range that the crosstable is constructed with – xref  (to be referenced or constructed)  - name for the crosstable that is constructed – also the 3 columns from the xref table that are used in the crosstable – entity 1, entity 2, text (optional). If no text is referenced (the crosstable just communicates relation yes / no a text field (X or YES or TRUE) must be chosen.
meta_output_content: The 2 entities that are involved in the relation, the rows and collumsn that were selected for the corsstable – xref   - crosstable –  3 columns from the xref table
meta_output_loss_risks: resolving chnages back to the xref might become impossible

name: crosstable_xref
details: write the crosstable edits back to the xref representation
frames_in: crosstable
frames_out: The relation table in xref form is updated – potential additional columns are left untouched
meta_or_config_input: policy for writeback – DROP_EMPTY etc.

name: expand_multiaxis
details: combines a sequence of projections that construct a multiaxis crosstable projection. Depending on the config some of these blocks may not be executed. (cell-codec / xref_crosstable / enrich helpers)
frames_in: defined by sum of transformations involved in the wrapper
frames_out: defined by sum of transformations involved in the wrapper
frames_consumed: defined by sum of transformations involved in the wrapper
meta_or_config_input: defined by sum of transformations involved in the wrapper plus potential switches to enable / disable steps

name: compact_multiaxis
details: combines a sequence of inverse projections that deconstruct a multiaxis crosstable
frames_in: crosstable

name: add_legend
details: adds a legend to a presentation, depending on configuration restricting values in the target cells. Entries can be taken from config or other frames
frames_in: potentially frames referenced by the legend and the data frame
frames_out: No data is changed – the legend is rendered during the render step

name: drop_legend
details: removes a previously added legend anchor from meta so the render layer no longer composes a legend block next to the table; payload frames are unchanged
frames_in: the data frame and any legend-source frame previously composed onto one workbook page (reference only; payload is not consumed)
frames_out: no payload frames are changed; removing the meta legend anchor changes only how the render layer composes the workbook page
meta_workflow_prerequisites: a prior add_legend anchored a legend relation in meta (presentation intent); legend identity and durable entries live outside _meta (config- or frame-backed)
meta_output_loss_risks: losing the legend anchor only drops the rendered legend composition on the next render (presentation continuity), not payload data. Keeping a rendered legend block out of payload on re-import is a separate workbook read-path classification concern, not a persistence-time prune by this step.
notes: drop_legend is a meta-only transformation: it operates on _meta (part of the frames array) and leaves normal/payload frames unchanged (operation_locus=meta_only). Meta transformation is common to many steps; the distinguishing trait here is that payload frames are untouched. Legend vocabulary is also an allowed-value source for validation/cell-codec (conceptual overlap). Per review (2026-07-03): the 'legend surface must not reimport as payload' concern belongs to the workbook read path, distinct from this step and from any persistence-time prune; cold-storage lifecycle notes (TLIFE-DROP-LEGEND-*) blur these loci - flagged, not reconciled.

name: add_table
details: add a table and create appropriate metadata
frames_in: workbook frame set
frames_out: workbook frame set with the added frame/table
meta_workflow_prerequisites: schema-relevant metadata (frame/column schema, key declarations) owned by the schema-maintenance contract
meta_or_config_input: target table name and initial schema/columns to create
meta_output_content: schema metadata registering the new frame/table so later steps can consume it
meta_output_consumer: later transformations/validations that require the frame to exist (FK helpers, XRef, validation)
notes: Conceptual (cold-storage inquiry, not yet contract text); observed schema maintenance is column-level only (add/drop/rename/reorder column in src/spreadsheet_handling/domain/schema_maintenance/operations.py) with no table/key operation.

name: remove_table
details: remove a table and delete appropriate metadata
frames_in: workbook frame set
frames_out: workbook frame set without the removed frame/table
meta_workflow_prerequisites: schema-relevant metadata and references (FK/XRef/validation) that may name the removed frame
meta_or_config_input: target table name to remove
meta_output_content: schema metadata with the frame/table declaration removed; dangling references may remain for validation to report
meta_output_consumer: validation and later steps that referenced the frame
meta_output_loss_risks: references (FK policy, XRef axis, validation, presentation) that still name the removed frame become dangling
notes: Conceptual (cold-storage inquiry, not yet contract text); observed schema maintenance is column-level only (add/drop/rename/reorder column in src/spreadsheet_handling/domain/schema_maintenance/operations.py) with no table/key operation.

name: rename_table
details: rename a table and keep metadata consistent
frames_in: the frame/table to rename
frames_out: the renamed frame/table
meta_workflow_prerequisites: schema-relevant metadata that addresses the frame by name; explicit rename preserves identity that a raw spreadsheet rename would break (delete+add)
meta_or_config_input: old and new table name
meta_output_content: schema metadata updated so references to the frame follow the rename where the operation can do so deterministically
meta_output_consumer: later steps addressing the frame by name
meta_output_loss_risks: references outside the schema-maintenance contract (pipeline YAML, workbook views) are not guaranteed to be updated
notes: Conceptual (cold-storage inquiry, not yet contract text); observed schema maintenance is column-level only (add/drop/rename/reorder column in src/spreadsheet_handling/domain/schema_maintenance/operations.py) with no table/key operation.

name: rename_key
details: rename a PK and keep all refeences consistent
frames_in: the entity frame holding the key column, plus frames referencing that key (FK columns, XRef relation tuple frames)
frames_out: the same frames with the key value renamed and known references updated
meta_workflow_prerequisites: declared primary key and known references (FK relations, XRef axes, schema-relevant metadata addressing the key value)
meta_or_config_input: target frame/key, old->new key value(s) (mapping table preferred over regex); optional dry-run/report and reference policy
meta_output_content: updated key declarations and reference metadata; report of references that could not be updated
meta_output_consumer: FK helpers, XRef, validation, and future record-addressed metadata
meta_output_loss_risks: unupdated references become dangling; defensive merge of stale references can resurrect zombie key values
notes: Conceptual (cold-storage inquiry, not yet contract text); observed schema maintenance is column-level only (add/drop/rename/reorder column in src/spreadsheet_handling/domain/schema_maintenance/operations.py) with no table/key operation. Distinct from TRANS-QUERY-REPLACE: rename_key updates references and aims for consistency, query_replace edits payload values directly with user-owned roundtrip integrity.

name: validate_integrity
details: prevent dangling references, ensure pk uniques.
frames_in: frames to check (entity frames and relation/reference frames)
frames_out: input frames unchanged; validation findings produced
meta_workflow_prerequisites: declared primary-key/uniqueness rules and foreign-key/reference rules; optional severity/mode (warning vs blocking) policy
meta_or_config_input: which checks to run and whether findings warn or fail
meta_output_content: validation findings (e.g. duplicate primary keys, dangling references) surfaced as data/report
meta_output_consumer: the pipeline/CI gate and the user reviewing findings
notes: Reference/uniqueness/graph validation is implemented (validate_references, validate_graph, validate_fk_helpers) but not under a single 'validate_integrity' step; overlaps the broader validation capability family.

name: extend_xref_existence_attribute
details: if a crosstable is not supposed to carry information just mark the relation of the enities, this transformation can acs a pseudo “value” to a pair of values creating the trippel needed for xref_crosstable
frames_in: a relation in xref/tuple form expressing existence-only pairs
frames_out: the relation extended with a pseudo existence value so each pair becomes a (row, column, value) triple consumable by xref_crosstable
meta_workflow_prerequisites: the two axis entities and the pair relation; xref projection intent
meta_or_config_input: the pseudo value/token used to represent existence (e.g. X, TRUE)
meta_output_content: record of the injected existence attribute so the inverse can drop it
notes: Not observed as a registered step; conceptual support projection for existence-only XRef relations. Value-token meaning is owned by legend/cell-codec, not XRef (boundary).

name: drop_xref_existence_attribute
details: to create a pair the third value of the tripple is dropped, which means user captured values are lost, just their existence is carried allong
frames_in: a relation triple whose value column is an existence marker
frames_out: the relation reduced to existence-only pairs
meta_workflow_prerequisites: evidence that the value column is an injected existence marker, not user payload
meta_output_loss_risks: intentionally lossy: any real cell payload is discarded, so it must only be applied to existence markers
notes: Not observed as a registered step; conceptual inverse of the existence-attribute extension.

name: adjust_presentation_names
details: Presentation names of tables and columns can be adjusted. Either by explicitly providing names to be used in spreadsheet presentation, that differ from the filenames / json field names or by applying a policy (perhaps configurable), e.g. transform _ in names to widespaces, capitalize first letters etc.
meta_or_config_input: explicit display names per table/column, or a naming policy (e.g. underscores to spaces, capitalize first letters)
meta_output_content: display-name presentation intent for tables/columns, distinct from logical frame/field names
meta_output_consumer: the render layer / workbook write path
meta_output_loss_risks: loss only reverts display to logical names; presentation continuity, not a data-integrity risk
notes: Weak/indirect evidence; no implementation observed. Functional model (ch05/05) treats workbook presentation as a lifecycle attribute, not a domain transformation - potential tension with modelling name adjustment as a transformation. frames_in/out left empty (no payload change).

name: query_replace
details: Influnece data directly (e.g. to change “” to “n.a.” for a certain column when changing from structured data to presentation data) the opposite direction can be relalised by an  adjusted query-replace but round trip integrity is user repsonsibliity.
frames_in: the frame(s)/column(s) whose payload values are edited
frames_out: the same frames with matched values replaced in place
meta_or_config_input: target scope (frame/column), match pattern, replacement value
meta_output_loss_risks: not a reversible projection; roundtrip integrity is the pipeline author's responsibility (the reverse is a separate configured query-replace)
notes: No dedicated functional-model source found; direct payload-adjustment step. No implementation observed. Distinct from schema maintenance/rename_key: edits values directly, does not update references or guarantee reversibility.

name: bootstrap
details: a generic way to enter any metadata to the pipeline. There is a way to differentiate between the intent to overwrite existing meta or to provide a default only for missing meta.
frames_in: working frames after boundary ingress
frames_out: the same working frames with internal _meta attached/merged
meta_workflow_prerequisites: structured data may enter without trusted _meta after crossing the application boundary
meta_or_config_input: a meta-shaped declaration to merge (schema/key declarations, presentation defaults, validation/view defaults) plus precedence mode: fill-if-absent vs overwrite
meta_output_content: declared meta merged into working meta using project precedence rules; field meaning stays owned by each field's own contract
meta_output_consumer: later transformations that require the declared intent (FK helpers, XRef, validation, render)
meta_output_loss_risks: configured as overwrite it can clobber user-carried presentation intent from a prior roundtrip; fill-if-absent preserves it

name: prune
details: a way to delete  data from meta – used to clear information that is not relevant beyond the scope of the pipeline at its end to prevent accumulation of meta.
meta_workflow_prerequisites: transient, run-local, or projection-scope meta that inverse projections could not fully consume
meta_or_config_input: which meta keys/classes to prune before a structured-data egress/boundary
meta_output_content: removes non-durable meta so it does not accumulate or reimport as stale across the boundary
meta_output_consumer: the persistence boundary / next forward run
meta_output_loss_risks: pruning meta still needed by a not-yet-run inverse projection loses recomposition scope; sequence prune after inverse steps
notes: Functional-model concept (boundary pruning); no registered prune pipeline step observed today (ch05/03 states this explicitly). Row's pipeline_exposed=true conflicts with 'not implemented' - flagged, not changed.

name: split_by_discriminator
details: splits a table by the value of one coumn into separate shards. Can be used for storage sharding (useful to avoid mege conflicts) or to  create similar xrefs of 2 entities for each of the value – in this case the respective column acts as a third dimension of the formerly 2d cross table
frames_in: one source frame containing a discriminator column
frames_out: one frame per discriminator value (per-value shards)
meta_workflow_prerequisites: the discriminator column; optional row-order preservation
meta_or_config_input: source frame, discriminator column, naming for per-value frames
meta_output_content: split mapping recorded at _meta.split_by_discriminator (operation, discriminator column, per-value frames) so merge_by_discriminator can restore the discriminator [observed]
meta_output_loss_risks: losing the split mapping prevents restoring the discriminator value on merge
notes: split/merge steps and the _meta.split_by_discriminator path are implementation-observed; frame/meta IO wording is inferred. Cold-storage conditional-follow-up derived views are a separate, non-MVP concept - do not conflate.

name: merge_by_discriminator
details: merge a set of tables by discriminator
frames_in: the per-discriminator frames
frames_out: one merged frame with the discriminator column restored
meta_workflow_prerequisites: the split mapping written by split_by_discriminator (_meta.split_by_discriminator) [observed]

name: merge_list_to_separated
details: read a list value (as it occures in structured data like json) and convert into one merged value that contains a list of strings separated by a configurable separator. Provide an option to process list of pairs by using 2 separators (“key1” , “value1” / “key2” , “value2”)
frames_in: a frame with a column holding a structured list value (e.g. from json)
frames_out: the same frame with the list rendered as one separator-joined cell value
meta_or_config_input: the separator(s); optional pair mode using two separators (key/value)
meta_output_content: the separator/encoding choice needed to reverse the join
meta_output_loss_risks: if the separator choice is lost or the payload contains the separator, the split may be ambiguous or irreversible
notes: Not MVP / not implemented (nested-list strategies are future concepts). Overlaps cell-codec / cell-embedded-list boundary.

name: split_list_to_separated
details: transform the list of separated strings back to the list form so it can be stored in json as a nested list
frames_in: a frame with a separator-joined cell value
frames_out: the same frame with the value parsed back into a structured list for nested (json) storage
notes: Not MVP / not implemented (nested-list strategies are future concepts). Overlaps cell-codec / cell-embedded-list boundary. Name 'split_list_to_separated' appears mismatched to the id (expected split_separated_to_list) and collides with TRANS-SPLIT-XREF-TO-SEPARATED's name - flagged, not changed.

name: merge_list_to_separated
details: read a column that contains n values separated by a configurable separator and asumming the resulting strings are foreign keys for another table, construct an xref  between this entity and the entity refrenced by the foreign key (tupple representation). Either use a pair of both entities or use a tripple if the separated values are not only the keys but keys with an additional string.
frames_in: an entity frame with a column of separator-joined foreign keys
frames_out: an xref/tuple relation between this entity and the referenced entity (pair, or triple when an extra token is present)
meta_workflow_prerequisites: which column carries the keys, the referenced entity/target frame, and the separator
meta_or_config_input: separator; referenced entity/key; pair vs triple mode
meta_output_content: mapping needed to resolve the xref back to the separated column
notes: Not MVP / not implemented (nested-list strategies are future concepts). Overlaps cell-codec / cell-embedded-list boundary. Name field is 'merge_list_to_separated', duplicating TRANS-MERGE-LIST-TO-SEPARATED - copy/paste error flagged, not changed. Overlaps XRef and separated-list boundaries.

name: split_list_to_separated
details: take an xref and resolve the relation back to one (or both) of the entitiy talbes involved representing the opposing relation to a  separated list.
frames_in: an xref/tuple relation
frames_out: an entity frame with the opposing relation rendered as a separator-joined column
meta_workflow_prerequisites: the separator and which side of the relation to project
notes: Not MVP / not implemented (nested-list strategies are future concepts). Overlaps cell-codec / cell-embedded-list boundary. Name 'split_list_to_separated' duplicates TRANS-SPLIT-SEPARATED-TO-LIST - flagged, not changed.

name: flatten_object_to_column
details: read a column that contains a string serialization of an object and deserialize it so it can be wirtten as a nested json.
frames_in: a frame with a column holding a string-serialized object
frames_out: the same frame/record with the object deserialized for nested (json) storage
meta_or_config_input: the serialization format/codec
notes: Not MVP/implemented. inverse_transformation_id points to TRANS-SPLIT-XREF-TO-SEPARATED, which looks wrong - expected inverse is TRANS-UNFLATTEN_COLUMN_TO_OBJECT. Also name (flatten_object_to_column) vs details (deserialize string->object) vs type (projection-inverse) appear inconsistent (details read as unflatten). Flagged, not changed. Overlaps cell-codec (cell-embedded object) and header_shape (flatten/unflatten multi-level headers, implemented separately as flatten_headers/unflatten_headers).

name: unflatten_column_to_object
details: read a column that contains an object and serialize it as a string that is stored in one column so it can be represented in a spreadsheet
frames_in: a frame/record with a nested object
frames_out: the same frame with the object serialized into one string column for spreadsheet representation
meta_or_config_input: the serialization format/codec
meta_output_content: the codec/format needed to deserialize on the reverse path
notes: Not MVP/implemented. inverse_transformation_id points to TRANS-SEPARATED-TO-XREF, which looks wrong - expected inverse is TRANS-FLATTEN_OBJECT_TO_COLUMN. Name (unflatten_column_to_object) vs details (serialize object->string) vs type (projection) appear inconsistent (details read as flatten). Flagged, not changed. See TRANS-FLATTEN_OBJECT_TO_COLUMN note.

name: drop_frames
details: drop a frame as part of the pipeline. This changed payload data directly. A user that designes a pipeline can thus delete frames he generated – perhaps as a report rather than an editing surface. The responsibility to avoid loosing important data lies with the pipeline designer.

name: Change-schema-columns
details: offer a range of operations (as parameters) that influence the canonical schema
frames_in: workbook frame set
frames_out: workbook frame set with the changed columns
meta_output_content: meta references of the column will be adjusted in case of a rename and pruned in case of a delete etc.

name: flatten_headers
details: read a header that contains multiple levels and flatten it
notes: used to work with io_adapters that cannot represent multiheaders

name: unflatten_headers
details: transform a flattened header to a classic multiheader representation
notes: used to work with io_adapters that cannot represent multiheaders
